hooks.prompt: A Claude Code session is starting with the powerloom-home plugin loaded. Greet the user by invoking the `powerloom_whoami` MCP tool to confirm the local home backend is live. Report the db_path + version. If `weave` is on PATH and `weave auth whoami` succeeds, also mention the hosted Powerloom identity in one line. Keep the greeting to 2-4 lines total — don't drown the session-start in status.

hooks.command: bash -c 'f="$CLAUDE_TOOL_PATH"; if [ -z "$f" ] || [ ! -f "$f" ]; then exit 0; fi; case "$f" in *.yaml|*.yml) if head -2 "$f" | grep -q "^apiVersion:.*powerloom\.app"; then echo "[powerloom-home hook] Detected Powerloom manifest: $f"; if command -v weave >/dev/null 2>&1; then weave plan "$f" 2>&1 | head -40; else echo "(weave not on PATH — skipping plan preview)"; fi; fi;; esac'
